spec_match.rationale: Spec's per-track surface (label, settings, displaySurface, isPrimary) is precisely what the page enumerates per track via track.getSettings().

interactive.rationale: Probe button calls the API and renders a per-track card with label/readyState/displaySurface/resolution. Stop button releases all tracks. Real degradation message on rejection.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises 'per-screen settings and a primary flag — probe what your environment returns' and that is exactly the deliverable.

edge_cases.rationale: isPrimary is faked: card flags track index 0 as primary on `displaySurface === 'monitor'` rather than reading an actual spec field. There is no real `isPrimary` track attribute in the WICG draft. This may mislead visitors about the platform contract.

references.rationale: See also links the chromestatus entry and the WICG spec.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, no static-card pattern, badge override has both background and color set.

openQuestions.title: Verify the source of `isPrimary` against the spec
openQuestions.detail: The card emits a `primary` badge for track index 0 if displaySurface === 'monitor', but the WICG draft does not currently expose `isPrimary` as a MediaStreamTrack attribute. Confirm the actual signal (likely via Window Management API `getScreenDetails().screens[i].isPrimary` matched by deviceId), and update the demo to read the real field rather than guess by index — otherwise the demo teaches a false API.

openQuestions.title: Add a deviceId stability probe
openQuestions.detail: The spec promises stable deviceIds across captures so compliance recorders can re-identify monitors. Add a 'capture twice and compare' button that asserts deviceIds match across two consecutive calls.

openQuestions.title: Show the `applyConstraints` failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Per-screen track-level constraints (e.g. requesting a different displaySurface) usually reject. A short demo of `applyConstraints` succeeding for width/height and failing for displaySurface would teach the affordance.

summary: Strong per-track inspector that hits the spec accurately except for the `isPrimary` heuristic, which appears to be fabricated rather than read from the platform. Worth fixing because the page is otherwise authoritative.
